prompt.content: A student says, “A circle is a round shape.” Why is this definition not mathematically precise?

choices.content: It does not mention equal side lengths.
It does not describe a circle as points in a plane that are the same distance from a fixed point.
It only describes circles that have a radius of 1.
It describes a line segment instead of a circle.

steps.content: “Round shape” is informal. A precise definition of a circle uses points in a plane that are all the same distance from a fixed point.
